danger_signals.visual_form: 2D sprite demons and possessed undead humans rendered at fixed angles
Multiple sprite enemies clustered or approaching simultaneously
Pits and pools of waste material in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; requires avoidance or radiation suit protection

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated ceiling descent in level architecture
danger_signals.meaning: Crushing hazard; time-limited threat requiring movement
Progression blocked; keycard or switch required

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger or distinct demon sprite at level terminus
danger_signals.meaning: Climactic encounter; level completion blocked until defeated

safety_signals.visual_form: Marked room location within labyrinthine level
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage to next level; level completion objective

safety_signals.visual_form: Armament items in level or dropped by defeated enemies

safety_signals.meaning: Toxic waste immunity; enables safe passage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Space marine military aesthetic; chainsaw, shotgun, plasma rifle weaponry
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Possessed undead humans with military/human appearance; may carry weapons
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military bases on moons of Mars; technological installations; military infrastructure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Demons; supernatural creatures; grotesque sprite designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple demon types with distinct visual forms; horn/claw designs; magical attack capabilities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hell dimension locations; supernatural/infernal aesthetic; demonic architecture

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Technological military architecture; human and possessed human enemies predominate
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies show human-like movement patterns; mixed melee and ranged attacks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Infernal/supernatural environment; pure demonic enemies; hellish aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Demons use magical attacks (fireballs); faster, more aggressive behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Larger enemy groups; denser sprite clustering
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies move toward player on sight/sound; attack speed increases with difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy sprite density increases; no visual indicator of difficulty
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies move faster; attack more frequently; increased damage output

environmental_storytelling.technique: Architectural progression from technology to supernatural
environmental_storytelling.example: Mars military bases transition to Hell dimension, visualizing narrative shift from human conflict to demonic invasion
Possessed undead humans in military bases; pure demons in hell; indicates invasion origin and scale
Toxic waste pits in industrial areas; lowering ceilings in confined spaces; environmental design indicates human industrial presence corrupted by demonic influence
Final level of each episode culminates in single major boss fight; visual/mechanical emphasis marks narrative progression gates

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationary or wandering within patrol area
Direct movement toward player; initiate melee or magical attack
Bite, claw, or gunfire attacks; human-like movement speed
Enhanced aggression; larger sprite; heightened attack frequency and damage
Enemies react faster; reduced reaction time to player actions

notes: DOOM (SNES 1995) uses 2.5D graphics (3D environment + 2D sprite enemies) to communicate world state primarily through enemy type placement, environmental hazard visual presentation, and architectural design. Faction identity is implicit rather than explicit—human vs. demonic distinction carries through enemy sprite design and environmental aesthetic. No color-coded faction systems or symbolic sigils present; communication relies on creature design and context. World state progression (Mars→Hell) manifests through environmental visual transition. Difficulty affects behavioral manifestation (enemy speed/aggression) without visual environmental change.
